arts.sum: Enzo Maresca will have been delighted with how his Chelsea players ended their Conference League group stage campaign at Stamford Bridge last night. The Blues...

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Enzo Maresca confirms who Blues will sell in January
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has made a flying start as Chelsea manager, with the Blues’ excellent form under the former Leicester boss continuing as they beat Shamrock Rovers 5-1 in the Conference League on Thursday. But Maresca also knows that his squad will change during the January transfer window, with the Italian admitting that several players are likely to depart Stamford Bridge in that time, including potentially Christopher Nkunku .

arts.ttl: Report: Chelsea now want to sign one of Pep Guardiola's 'favourite' players from Barcelona
arts.sum: They can get him for just £20m

arts.ttl: 'Brilliant': Jason Cundy hails 'quick' Chelsea player for his display in the 5-1 win over Shamrock Rovers
arts.sum: Chelsea’s superb form at the back end of 2024 continued yesterday with a convincing victory over Shamrock Rovers. Chelsea produced another clinical display on Thursday...

arts.ttl: Christopher Nkunku drops early Chelsea team news hint as Enzo Maresca issue solved
arts.sum: Now, with European regular Nkunku expected to play against Shamrock, what is - in effect - a third team for as many matches is expected to be deployed. Astana and the next game - a Premier League date with Brentford - came and went with two victories for Chelsea as the future and present helped keep the momentum going.

arts.ttl: 'How have Barca let him go?' Meet Chelsea's 18-year-old future 'star'
arts.sum: Marc Guiu, 18, could be a "big star" for Chelsea after scoring a hat-trick against Shamrock Rovers in the Conference League.

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall could leave in January, admits Enzo Maresca
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca admits that there are no guarantees that Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall will spend the second half of the season at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
arts.sum: Bournemouth look to repeat Old Trafford heroics, Saints must get ugly and it’s Füllkrug time for West Ham Approaching the halfway point, it is hard to gauge where Aston Villa are at. Regardless of Manchester City’s extended wobble, a result at home to Pep Guardiola’s side will go a long way to providing an indication. It was always going to be a big ask for Villa to better or even equal last season’s achievements but, with automatic qualification to the Champions League last 16 a distinct possibility and a top-four finish within reach, it has been another solid, if imperfect, start. Only once this season have Villa recorded victory after a European night, in September after winning at Young Boys. This time last season Villa were third in the division, two points off the summit, on 35 points. They may be 10 points worse off now but, despite defeat at Nottingham Forest last time out, they remain in a good spot. Ben Fisher Aston Villa v Manchester City, Saturday 12.30pm (all times GMT) Brentford v Nottingham Forest, Saturday 3pm Ipswich v Newcastle, Saturday 3pm West Ham v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Crystal Palace v Arsenal, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Chelsea youngster Marc Guiu 'can go all the way', insists Joe Cole after his first-half hat-trick as former Blues star insists 'Barcelona have made a real mistake' letting him go
arts.sum: After emerging from the Catalan club's famed La Masia academy, Guiu arrived at Stamford Bridge in July after the Blues triggered the £5m release clause in his contract.
